concerns.text: T013 (CLI entry point) depends on T014 (environment logging), but T014 is listed AFTER T013 in the task sequence. The CLI must configure the environment logger before the logger can be invoked; T014 must precede T013 to satisfy the producer-consumer flow.

T016 (Calibration) is listed under US2 but logically produces the 'calibrated_traces.csv' artifact consumed by T021 (Kinetic Fit). T016 must be explicitly ordered before T021. Currently, T021 is listed before T016, violating the data-flow dependency.

T030 (Correlation) consumes outputs from T029/T029b (DFT parsing). While the 'Critical Execution Order' section notes this, the task list itself places T030 before T029b in the visual sequence. T029b must be listed before T030 to ensure the 'Producer before Consumer' rule is visually and logically clear in the task list.
T012 (Integration test) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T014 implementation. A test task cannot run in parallel with the implementation it validates. Remove [P] tag from T012.

T039 (Full pipeline integration test) depends on T026 and T034. T026 (US2 output) and T034 (US3 output) are in different phases. Ensure T039 is strictly ordered after the completion of Phase 5 (T034) and Phase 4 (T026) in the execution logic, not just listed in Phase 6.
